hint: être = to be. je suis, tu es, il est.
explain: The chant: je suis · tu es · il est.

hint: avoir = to have. j'ai, tu as, il a.
explain: tu as → il a: the il-form is the tu-form minus s.

hint: aller = to go. je vais, tu vas, il va.
explain: Same trick: tu vas → il va.

hint: vouloir = to want. je veux, tu veux, il veut — x, x, t.
explain: All three sound /vø/ — only the last letter changes. This one has 9 lapses on your card; let's retire it.

hint: pouvoir = can. je peux, tu peux, il peut — same x/x/t family as vouloir.
explain: vouloir and pouvoir rhyme in every form.

prompt: One letter apart: “il a” vs “il va” —
options: il a = he HAS (avoir) · il va = he GOES (aller)
explain: Your writings mix these. a = has, va = goes. Say them aloud: /a/ vs /va/.

prompt: Say: “He has a coffee.” (has — which verb?)
hint: has → avoir → il a (NOT il va).
explain: il a un café. il va would mean he GOES.
elle va à Paris — going, so va.

prompt: The blur to kill: je suis / je vais / je veux =
options: I am / I go / I want — three different verbs
three ways to say I am
present, past, future of one verb
explain: suis = am (être) · vais = go (aller) · veux = want (vouloir). Your morning chant keeps them apart.
